topics.title: Notice at Collection and Privacy Disclosures
topics.summary: When and how a business must disclose categories, purposes, sale/share status, retention, and rights at or before collection.
answer_cautions: Do not substitute an OAG FAQ for the statute or CPPA regulations.
Separate CCPA notice-at-collection duties from CalOPPA privacy-policy posting duties.
comparative_hooks: PIPA privacy policy and collection notice rules

topics.title: Opt Out of Sale/Share and GPC
topics.summary: Consumer opt-out rights for sale/share, opt-out links, opt-out preference signals, and frictionless processing.
answer_cautions: Do not call enforcement settlements judicial precedent.
Check whether the issue is sale, share, or both.

topics.title: Sensitive Personal Information and Limit Use
topics.summary: Definition and limit-use framework for sensitive personal information, including notice and risk-assessment overlap.
answer_cautions: Medical or health-related data may also require CMIA analysis.
Private-right-of-action analysis is narrower than general CCPA compliance analysis.

topics.title: Data Minimization and Purpose Limitation
topics.summary: Reasonable, necessary, and proportionate collection/use/retention under the CCPA and CPPA regulations.
answer_cautions: Tie the answer to disclosed purposes and consumer reasonable expectations.
Avoid saying California uses GDPR terminology unless comparing frameworks.
comparative_hooks: PIPA purpose limitation and minimum collection

topics.title: Service Provider, Contractor, and Third Party Contracts
topics.summary: Contract requirements, restrictions, and downstream processing duties for service providers and contractors.
answer_cautions: Do not assume a vendor is a service provider without the required contractual restrictions.
Marketing cooperative disclosures may implicate sale analysis.

topics.title: Data Breach and CCPA Private Right of Action
topics.summary: Reasonable security, California breach-notice statutes, and the limited CCPA private right of action.
answer_cautions: The CCPA private right of action is narrower than general CCPA violations.
Federal district court decisions are persuasive, not California appellate precedent.

topics.title: Risk Assessments
topics.summary: When a business must conduct risk assessments and what the assessment must include under 2026 CPPA regulations.
answer_cautions: Use the 2026 effective regulation text and retrieved date.
Do not overstate older CCPA materials as containing the 2026 risk-assessment details.

topics.title: Automated Decisionmaking Technology
topics.summary: ADMT notices, opt-out/access rights, appeals, and exceptions under the 2026 CPPA regulations.
answer_cautions: The ADMT rules are part of the 2026 regulation package.
Check exceptions before saying opt-out is always required.

topics.title: Minors and Children
topics.summary: CCPA opt-in rules for minors, child-directed enforcement, SOPIPA, and Age-Appropriate Design Code issues.
answer_cautions: Age-Appropriate Design Code obligations require current litigation/injunction status checks.
COPPA may also apply but is not part of this California KB.

topics.title: CIPA Website Tracking, Chat, and Session Replay
topics.summary: California Invasion of Privacy Act issues around wiretapping, eavesdropping, call recording, chat widgets, pixels, and session replay.
answer_cautions: CIPA is adjacent California privacy law, not CCPA.
Publication and authority level vary sharply between California Supreme Court, Ninth Circuit memorandum, and district court orders.

topics.title: CIPA Pen Register, Pixels, Cookies, and IP Address Tracking
topics.summary: California CIPA pen-register/trap-and-trace theories involving pixels, cookies, IP addresses, tracking scripts, and ad-tech routing data.
answer_cautions: CIPA pen-register cases are fast-moving and often federal district court persuasive authority.
Separate section 631 wiretap/eavesdropping theories from sections 638.50 and 638.51 pen-register theories.
comparative_hooks: PIPA behavioral advertising and tracking consent

topics.title: Data Brokers and Delete Act
topics.summary: California data broker registration and deletion mechanism provisions collected from LegInfo.
answer_cautions: Do not collapse data broker duties into ordinary CCPA business duties.
Check current CPPA rulemaking and implementation status for operational details.
comparative_hooks: PIPA personal information file registration/comparable governance

topics.title: OAG/CPPA Enforcement Authority and Administrative Materials
topics.summary: Civil and administrative enforcement powers, investigation rules, and how to use enforcement materials as nonprecedential authority.
answer_cautions: Administrative/enforcement material is not judicial precedent.
Distinguish press releases from complaints, stipulated judgments, and final agency orders.
comparative_hooks: PIPA regulator corrective orders and penalties

golden_questions.question: A California website collects email addresses and location data before account signup. What local KB authorities should an answer cite for notice-at-collection duties?
must_not: Treat CalOPPA as the only source of CCPA notice duties.

golden_questions.question: Does a business that sells or shares personal information need to process Global Privacy Control or a similar opt-out preference signal?
must_not: Call Sephora or Honda binding judicial precedent.

golden_questions.question: A health content site shares browsing activity with ad-tech vendors. Which authorities support sensitive personal information and limit-use analysis?
must_not: Ignore CMIA when the facts involve medical information.

golden_questions.question: What authorities govern whether collecting precise geolocation is reasonably necessary and proportionate?
must_not: Describe the rule only as GDPR data minimization without California citations.

golden_questions.question: How should an answer analyze whether a vendor is a service provider rather than a third party?
must_not: Assume service-provider status without contract restrictions.

golden_questions.question: Can a consumer sue under the CCPA for any privacy violation, or only certain data-breach violations?
must_not: State that all CCPA violations create a private right of action.

golden_questions.question: When must a business conduct a California CCPA risk assessment under the 2026 rules?
must_not: Use pre-2026 materials as the only authority for detailed risk-assessment duties.

golden_questions.question: What should the KB cite for ADMT notice, opt-out, access, and appeal questions?
must_not: Treat ADMT as only a general AI governance issue without CCPA regulation citations.

golden_questions.question: How should an answer handle sale/share of minors' personal information and child-directed services?
must_not: State Age-Appropriate Design Code obligations without litigation-status caution.

golden_questions.question: A site uses a chat widget or session replay vendor. Which California authorities support CIPA analysis?
must_not: Present CIPA as CCPA or ignore authority-level differences among cases.

golden_questions.question: A website uses pixels or cookies that transmit IP addresses and routing data to ad-tech vendors. Which California authorities support CIPA pen-register analysis?
must_not: Conflate CIPA pen-register claims with CCPA sale/share opt-out claims.

golden_questions.question: What local sources should an answer cite for California data broker registration and deletion mechanism duties?
must_not: Collapse data broker duties into ordinary CCPA consumer request duties.

golden_questions.question: How should the agent use OAG settlements and CPPA administrative orders in a legal answer?
must_not: Treat administrative materials or press releases as binding judicial precedent.
